_patch_meta.description: Session B analytical write-backs for grace (reg 068): god_as_subject corrections, somatic_link updates, sb_classification, H2603B deletion, research flags

operations.description: G5485 charis — god_as_subject corrected to 1: God is named agent in majority of anchor verses (Eph 2:8, 2Cor 12:9, Rom 3:24, Gal 1:15, 1Cor 15:10 etc). Data gap confirmed in Session B Pass 2.
G5483 charizō — god_as_subject corrected to 1: Rom 8:32, 1Cor 2:12, Act 27:24, Gal 3:18 — God explicit subject of the grace-giving act.
G5487 charitoō — god_as_subject corrected to 1: Both NT occurrences (Luk 1:28, Eph 1:6) have God as agent. Term specifically names the act of being constituted as an object of divine grace.
H2580 chen — god_as_subject corrected to 1: 27 verses with divine reference; 'find favour in the eyes of the Lord' formula pervasive (Gen 6:8, Exo 33:17 etc).
H2587 channun — god_as_subject corrected to 1: All 13 occurrences in registry are divine character descriptions ('The Lord is gracious and merciful' — Exo 34:6 formula and parallels).
H2603A chanan — god_as_subject corrected to 1: 43 verses with divine reference; 'Be gracious to me, O Lord' is the dominant prayer form (Psa 6:2, 51:1, 67:1 etc).
H2580 chen — somatic_link set to 1: eyes/sight idiom ('find favour in the eyes of') pervasive across 28 somatic verses; face prominent (Exo 33:16, Num 6:25, Rut 2:10).

H2603A chanan — somatic_link set to 1: 24 somatic verses — prostration and kneeling (2Ki 1:13, Est 8:3), face (Num 6:25, Psa 67:1), heart (1Ki 8:47, Psa 119:58), eyes, bones (Psa 6:2), soul (Psa 31:9).
H8469 tachanum — somatic_link set to 1: 7 somatic verses — fasting, sackcloth, ashes, face (Dan 9:3), weeping (Jer 3:21, 31:9), raised hands (Psa 28:2), spirit (Zec 12:10).
H8467 techinnah — somatic_link set to 1: 8 somatic verses — hands stretched out (1Ki 8:38, 54; 2Ch 6:29), eyes (1Ki 8:52, 9:3), heart (1Ki 8:38, 9:3), knees (1Ki 8:54).

set.status_note: Phantom entry — no genuine verse occurrences. STEP data confirms all 72 verses fetched under H2603B resolve at span level to H2603A (span_code_found = H2603A in all 72 cases). H2603B is a theoretical lexicographical distinction with no recoverable textual presence in the STEP-tagged corpus. Deleted Session B 2026-04-09.
set.exclusion_reason: No corpus evidence — all span matches resolve to H2603A

operations.description: H2603B chanan (be loathsome) — status → delete. STEP analysis confirms zero genuine H2603B span hits across 72 verses; all resolve to H2603A. Data error in initial extraction now resolved.

set.sb_classification_reasoning: Grace originates at the spirit level — it is the freely given disposition of God toward humanity, received by the human person as a gift from above, not self-generated. It cannot arise from within the natural human capacity; its structure as gift requires an outside source. At the soul level, grace is thoroughly expressed: the desire for it, the fear of being without it, the experience of finding it, and the transformed inner disposition of the one who has lived under it are all soulish states — personally felt, emotionally coloured, relationally constituted. The somatic evidence (prostration, weeping, raised hands, fasting, gracious speech) confirms that grace is enacted through the body without originating there. The body enacts and externalises what is happening at the spirit-soul level. Confidence: high — pattern consistent across full verse set of 194 active verses and all nine owner terms.

operations.description: Registry 068 grace — sb_classification set to Spirit-soul interface; session_b_status advanced to Analysis Complete.

record.description: Grace-Forgiveness formal connection: G5483 charizō carries both 'give grace' and 'forgive/cancel debt' as primary senses. Eph 4:32 makes forgiveness the named expression of grace received. The grace-forgiveness relationship is formally embedded in the Greek term itself. Requires cross-registry analysis when forgiveness registry is complete.

operations.description: Research flag PH2-068-001: Grace-Forgiveness formal connection via G5483 charizō

record.description: Grace-Chesed boundary question: H2580 chen and chesed (steadfast love) travel together in OT formulas and verse pairs. Key structural question for Session D: does chen name the inner disposition of which chesed is the sustained relational expression? Cannot be resolved within this registry — requires chesed registry to be complete.

operations.description: Research flag PH2-068-002: Grace-Chesed boundary question — chen vs chesed structural relationship

record.description: Grace-Repentance sequence: Zec 12:10 (H8469 tachanum anchor) and Joel 2:13 (H2587 channun) together establish that grace precedes and enables the turn toward God — the capacity to repent is itself given as grace. This directional sequence (grace → repentance, not repentance → grace) requires confirmation against repentance registry data.

operations.description: Research flag PH2-068-003: Grace-Repentance sequence — grace as precondition for the turn

record.description: Grace-Identity formation signal: 1Cor 15:10 — 'by the grace of God I am what I am' — is the strongest statement in the corpus of grace as identity-constituting: not merely a gift received but the ground of being. Connects to identity-formation vocabulary. Flag for cross-registry investigation when identity vocabulary is processed.

operations.description: Research flag PH2-068-004: Grace-Identity formation — 1Cor 15:10 as identity-constituting signal

record.description: Grace-Lament connection: the weeping-supplication complex (tachanum + weeping in Jer 3:21, 31:9; Zec 12:10; Est 8:3; Dan 9:3) connects the grace-seeking posture to the lament tradition. Not currently in Section 5 connections list. Warrants investigation when lament vocabulary is processed.

operations.description: Research flag PH2-068-005: Grace-Lament connection via weeping-supplication complex

Session B analytical brief: wa-068-grace-session-b-brief-2026-04-09.md. Session C document updates required: Section 3 (13 verse annotations) and Section 4 (5 language annotations) → Session C v2 pending.

_patch_summary.notes: H8469 and H8467 god_as_subject retained at 0 — supplication terms, God is addressee not agent. H2603B deleted — zero genuine corpus span hits, all 72 verses resolve to H2603A.
